ddls:
CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "region_description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "customers" INTEGER /* Number of customers in the region */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the region */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the region */,
  "population_density" REAL /* Population density of the region */,
  "average_consumption" REAL /* Average power consumption in the region */,
  "number_of_substations" INTEGER /* Number of substations in the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "kpi_types" (
  "kpi_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each KPI type */,
  "kpi_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the KPI type (e.g., SAIDI, SAIFI, MAIFI) */,
  "kpi_type_description" TEXT /* Description of the KPI type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("kpi_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "time_periods" (
  "time_period_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each time period */,
  "time_period_name" TEXT /* Name of the time period (e.g., 2013, 1st Qtr 2016) */,
  "time_period_description" TEXT /* Description of the time period */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the time period */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the time period */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("time_period_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "kpi_data" (
  "kpi_data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each KPI data entry */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region */,
  "kpi_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the KPI type */,
  "time_period_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the time period */,
  "kpi_value" REAL /* Value of the KPI */,
  "substation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the substation */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the KPI data (e.g., real-time, historical) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("kpi_data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_kpi_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_kpi_data_time_period_id FOREIGN KEY ("time_period_id") REFERENCES time_periods ("time_period_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_kpi_data_substation_id FOREIGN KEY ("substation_id") REFERENCES substations ("substation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_kpi_data_kpi_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("kpi_type_id") REFERENCES kpi_types ("kpi_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interruption_causes" (
  "cause_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cause */,
  "cause_name" TEXT /* Name of the cause (e.g., Equipment Failure, Weather) */,
  "cause_description" TEXT /* Description of the cause */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cause_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interruption_data" (
  "interruption_data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interruption data entry */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region */,
  "time_period_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the time period */,
  "cause_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the cause */,
  "percentage" REAL /* Percentage of interruptions attributed to the cause */,
  "substation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the substation */,
  "number_of_interruptions" INTEGER /* Total number of interruptions for the cause */,
  "average_duration" REAL /* Average duration of interruptions for the cause */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the interruption data (e.g., real-time, historical) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interruption_data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interruption_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interruption_data_cause_id FOREIGN KEY ("cause_id") REFERENCES interruption_causes ("cause_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interruption_data_time_period_id FOREIGN KEY ("time_period_id") REFERENCES time_periods ("time_period_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interruption_data_substation_id FOREIGN KEY ("substation_id") REFERENCES substations ("substation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, manager, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "substations" (
  "substation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each substation */,
  "substation_name" TEXT /* Name of the substation */,
  "substation_description" TEXT /* Description of the substation */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region */,
  "location_latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the substation */,
  "location_longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the substation */,
  "number_of_transformers" INTEGER /* Number of transformers in the substation */,
  "total_capacity" REAL /* Total capacity of the substation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("substation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_substations_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance log */,
  "substation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the substation */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., preventive, corrective) */,
  "maintenance_description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the maintenance activity */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the maintenance activity */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance activity (e.g., scheduled, in progress, completed) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the maintenance activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_logs_substation_id FOREIGN KEY ("substation_id") REFERENCES substations ("substation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "third_party_activities" (
  "activity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each third-party activity */,
  "activity_name" TEXT /* Name of the activity */,
  "activity_description" TEXT /* Description of the activity */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the activity */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the activity */,
  "impact_level" TEXT /* Impact level of the activity (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("activity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_third_party_activities_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)
